semantic.role: CI/CD integration template for automated ArchSpine architecture sync and policy checking on GitLab Merge Requests
responsibilities: Provides a ready-to-use GitLab CI job (spine-check) that runs `spine sync --no-docs` and `spine check` on every MR
Defines prerequisites, environment variables (LLM_API_KEY, GITLAB_TOKEN), and customization points
Automatically posts an MR comment when architecture violations are detected and fails the pipeline
Captures check output as an artifact for debugging
outOfScope: Detailed ArchSpine configuration or init instructions
Support for other CI platforms (e.g., GitHub Actions, Jenkins)
Manual execution steps outside CI pipelines

English.purpose: This document provides a copy-paste GitLab CI template that lets teams enforce ArchSpine architecture rules automatically on every Merge Request. It eliminates the need to manually remember `spine check` and ensures violations are surfaced immediately in the MR discussion.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers and DevOps engineers managing a GitLab repository who have already initialized ArchSpine (`spine init`) and want to integrate architecture validation into their CI pipeline. Readers should be familiar with GitLab CI variables and rules.
key_takeaways: Drop the job into `.gitlab-ci.yml` and configure two CI variables (LLM_API_KEY + GITLAB_TOKEN) to start enforcing architecture checks
The job runs only on merge requests, so it does not slow down regular branch pipelines
On failure, a formatted comment is posted to the MR and the pipeline exits with status 1, blocking merge until violations are resolved
The output is saved as an artifact (`spine-check-output.txt`) for inspection when the job fails
